folio.label: 75r
folio.l: Who peynted the lyouö , telle me who
By god if a womman , had wryten stories
As Clerkes han , witú Inne here oratories
They wold han wretyn of men , more wykkednesse
Than al the mark of Adam , may redresse
The children of Mercurie , and of venus
Ben in here werkyngö , ful contrarious
Mercurie louetú wysdom , and science
And venus louetú riot , and despence
And for here dyuers , disposiciou~
Eche fayletú , in others exaltaciou~
As thus , god woot , Mercurie is desolate
In pisses , whan venus is exaltate
And venus falletú , there Mercurie is reised
Therfore no womman , of no Clerk is preysed
The Clerk whan he is old , & may nat do
Of venus werkes , wortú his olde shoo
Than syt he dounö , and wrytetú in his dotage
That wommen conne nout kepen , here mariage
But now to purpos , why I tolde the
That I was beten , for a book parde
Vp on a nygút , Iankyn that was oure Sire
Red on his book , as he sat by the fyre
Of Eue first , that for hire wykkednesse
Was almankynde brougút , to wrechednesse

note.notetxt: 694There is a cross in dry point in the left margin, it is not very clear and it is very small.ODR

folio.l: For whicú that iúu crist , hi~ self was slayn
That bougút vs , witú his hert blod a gayn
Loo heere expres of wo~men , may ©e fynde
That wo~man was the losse , of al mankynde
Tho redde he me , how Sampsou~ loost hise heeres
Slepynge his lemman , kytte he~ w hire sheres
Thurgú whicú tresouö , lost he bothe hise eyen
Tho redde he me , if that I shal nat lyen
Of Hercules , and of Dyanyre
That caused hi~ , to sette hi~ self a fyre
Nothyngö for gat he , the care and the woo
That Socrates had , witú hise wyues twoo
How Exantipa , cast pisse vpvp on hise hed
This sely man sat stille , as he were ded
He wypte his hed , nomore durst he seyn
But er that the thonder stynt , cometú reyn
Of Phasipha , that was the Quene of Creete
For shrewednesse hi~ thougút , the tale swete
Fy speke nomore , it is a grisly thyngö
Of hire horrible lust , & hire lykyngö
Of Clithermystra , for hire lecherie
That falsly mad hire husbonde , for to deye
